name: Deploy GitHub Pages green and verify vasudev.xyz online

description: Baseline local deploy readiness after resolving merge and removing Graphify graph

asi.hypothesis: A clean Hugo build with zero conflict markers and zero Graphify page/workflow references is ready to commit before pushing to GitHub Pages.
asi.local_checks: hugo --minify --gc passed, no unmerged files, no conflict markers, no blocked Graphify refs.

description: Attempted custom push command while autoresearch.sh gate was active

asi.hypothesis: Push and workflow monitoring can be treated as the deployment experiment, but the harness requires the command to be routed through autoresearch.sh when it exists.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change to keep. The run was rejected by the autoresearch harness before executing git push.
asi.next_action_hint: Update autoresearch.sh to support DEPLOY_REMOTE=1 mode, then run ./autoresearch.sh via run_experiment.

description: Remote deploy monitor selected a stale pre-fix workflow run

asi.hypothesis: Pushing current master then watching the latest deploy workflow should verify GitHub Pages, but the run selector must bind to the current HEAD SHA rather than any latest branch run.
asi.rollback_reason: The deployment check failed because it watched an older run that still contained the removed Graphify step, not because the current code failed.
asi.next_action_hint: Update autoresearch.sh to capture git rev-parse HEAD and select the Deploy to GitHub Pages run whose headSha matches it.

description: Watched current HEAD deploy workflow and verified live site HTTP 200

asi.hypothesis: Binding the workflow watcher to the current HEAD SHA avoids stale failed runs and correctly verifies the pushed deployment.

description: Pushed autoresearch watcher commit and verified deploy remained green

asi.hypothesis: Pushing the follow-up autoresearch watcher commit should not regress the Pages deploy or live site.
asi.live_site: https://vasudev.xyz/ returned HTTP 200 after the follow-up push.

description: Confirmed current HEAD deploy run and live site still green after compaction

asi.hypothesis: After resuming, the most valuable next check is binding the current local and remote HEAD to the already-triggered Pages run and confirming the live site remains online.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric equaled the existing best of zero deploy failures, so there was no code improvement to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing, extend the verification surface to include /blog/ and absence of /graph/ as diagnostic checks, but do not change production code unless a remote failure appears.
asi.workflow_run: 25226889617 is the Deploy to GitHub Pages run for current HEAD 4fd1ba1.

description: Extended verification to live blog and removed graph URL

asi.hypothesis: The next risk after home page HTTP 200 is that the blog route might not be online or the removed graph page might still be published from stale artifacts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at the existing best of zero deploy failures. The temporary benchmark expansion confirmed diagnostics but did not improve the primary metric.
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing, persist the /blog/ and /graph/ diagnostics only if the user wants stronger ongoing monitoring. Production remains green without code changes.
asi.removed_graph: https://vasudev.xyz/graph/ returned HTTP 404, confirming the graph page is no longer live.

description: Watched newest in-progress Pages run for current HEAD to completion

asi.hypothesis: A newer Pages run existed for the same HEAD after resume, so watching that run provides stronger evidence than relying on the earlier successful run.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric equaled best at zero deploy failures, so no code change should be kept.
asi.next_action_hint: Monitor for additional workflow_run-triggered Pages reruns from translation automation. If no new HEAD run appears, final state remains stable.
asi.live_site: home and blog returned 200; graph returned 404.

description: Updated persisted autoresearch notes and reconfirmed stable deploy state

asi.hypothesis: Persisting the resume findings in autoresearch.md helps future agents avoid rechecking stale-run and graph-removal questions, while the current deploy state should remain green.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric stayed at zero deploy failures. Autoresearch files are preserved, but no production code improvement was needed.
asi.next_action_hint: No failing deployment signal remains. If forced to continue, poll GitHub Actions for a new current-HEAD Pages run, otherwise stop changing production code.

description: Polled current deployed HEAD and live routes after second resume

asi.hypothesis: After the second resume, the next most valuable non-invasive experiment is to poll the current HEAD deployment and live routes without changing production code.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric equaled the existing best of zero deploy failures, so there was no production improvement to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: All deployment signals are stable. Continue only by monitoring for a new current-HEAD Pages run or a live-route regression; avoid changing production code unless a failure appears.

description: Blog-card diagnostic hit non-fast-forward remote before deploy verification

asi.hypothesis: Adding a live blog content diagnostic would verify the blog is not just HTTP 200 but actually rendering post cards.
asi.rollback_reason: The run failed before verification because origin/master advanced, likely from automation, so local HEAD must be synchronized before further deploy checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Fetch origin/master, inspect the new commit, rebase or fast-forward local master without reintroducing Graphify, then rerun ./autoresearch.sh.
asi.error_detail: git push rejected with non-fast-forward: remote contains work not present locally.

description: Remote OG automation commit broke Hugo with duplicate images front matter

asi.hypothesis: After fast-forwarding to the automation commit, the deploy loop should verify the new remote HEAD, but local Hugo build catches regressions before pushing.
asi.rollback_reason: Hugo build failed because the OG image automation commit added duplicate images keys to blog front matter.
asi.next_action_hint: Deduplicate images keys in English blog front matter and inspect the OG image workflow so it does not append duplicate images on future runs.
asi.error_detail: content/blog/frontend-ai-agents-streaming-ui.md had duplicate images keys at front matter lines 6 and 7.

description: Uncommitted fix could not trigger a Pages run because autoresearch pushes only commits

asi.hypothesis: Deduplicating front matter and fixing the OG generator should restore deploy green, but the experiment harness must push a commit for GitHub Actions to run.
asi.rollback_reason: The run failed because fixes were uncommitted, so git push had no new HEAD and no matching Pages workflow appeared.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the dedupe and generator fix, then use a keep log on a local readiness experiment or manually commit? In this harness, create a passing local run that can be kept/committed before remote verification.
asi.error_detail: No Deploy to GitHub Pages workflow run found after push.

description: Fix OG generator duplicate images regression and restore local deploy readiness

asi.hypothesis: The root cause is insertImageField inserting after description before seeing an existing images field later, so scanning for images first and replacing only the first occurrence prevents future duplicates while deduplicating current posts restores Hugo.
asi.root_cause: OG automation commit fbf3036 added duplicate images fields because insertImageField inserted after description and then also replaced the existing images field that followed description.
asi.fix: Deduplicated current English blog front matter, changed insertImageField to pre-detect images and drop duplicates, and added a regression test for images after description.
asi.verification: hugo --minify --gc passed and cd cmd/generate-og-images && go test ./... passed in autoresearch.sh local candidate mode.
asi.next_action_hint: Run ./autoresearch.sh again after this keep commit so it can push the committed fix and watch the matching GitHub Pages run.
